findings.summary: Settings is a single monolithic class that holds configuration for both OCR and classifier domains. Both domain packages depend on fields they do not use (e.g., classifier reads CLASSIFY_* fields while ocr reads OCR_DPI, OCR_MAX_SIDE, etc.), creating implicit coupling through a shared hub.
evidence: Settings class has 30+ fields mixing OCR-specific (OCR_DPI, OCR_MAX_SIDE, PAGE_WORKERS) and classifier-specific (CLASSIFY_PERSON_FIELD_ID, CLASSIFY_MAX_CHARS, CLASSIFY_TAXONOMY_LIMIT) concerns in a single class
Both daemon entry points import the same Settings and pass it through their entire call chain
findings.suggestion: Consider splitting Settings into a base section with shared fields and domain-specific sub-configs (OcrSettings, ClassifierSettings), or use Protocol types so each domain declares only the fields it needs.

findings.summary: common/tags.py pipeline_tag_ids() and clean_pipeline_tags() have knowledge of both OCR and classifier pipeline tag fields, coupling the shared layer to both domain-specific tag semantics.
evidence: pipeline_tag_ids() references PRE_TAG_ID, POST_TAG_ID, CLASSIFY_PRE_TAG_ID, OCR_PROCESSING_TAG_ID, CLASSIFY_PROCESSING_TAG_ID, CLASSIFY_POST_TAG_ID, and ERROR_TAG_ID -- enumerating all domain-specific tags in the shared layer
findings.suggestion: Have each domain register its own pipeline tag IDs rather than hard-coding them in the common layer, or accept tag IDs as parameters instead of reading them from Settings.

findings.summary: Pillow (PIL) is declared as a top-level project dependency but is only used by the OCR package (image_converter.py). The classifier daemon pulls in Pillow unnecessarily.
evidence: Only src/ocr/image_converter.py imports from PIL; no classifier or common module uses it
findings.suggestion: Move Pillow to an optional dependency group (e.g., [project.optional-dependencies] ocr = ['Pillow~=10.4']) so classifier-only deployments do not pull in the imaging library.

findings.summary: pdf2image is declared as a top-level dependency but is only used by the OCR pipeline for PDF-to-image conversion.
evidence: Only src/ocr/image_converter.py imports pdf2image; classifier modules do not use it
findings.suggestion: Group pdf2image with Pillow under an OCR optional dependency set.

findings.summary: common/__init__.py does not define __all__ or re-export any public surface, unlike ocr/ and classifier/ which both define explicit __all__ lists. This is an inconsistency across sibling packages.
evidence: common/__init__.py contains only a docstring: '"""Shared building blocks for the OCR and classification daemons."""'
ocr/__init__.py and classifier/__init__.py both re-export key symbols and define __all__
findings.suggestion: This is a minor inconsistency. Since common is used via direct submodule imports (from common.config import Settings) rather than package-level imports, the current approach is functional. Consider adding __all__ = [] to make the empty surface explicit.
